question:
Will Millwall FC win on 2025-11-08?

Description: In the upcoming game, scheduled for November 8, 2025
If Millwall FC wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

Resolution source: https://www.efl.com/

evidences.title: Millwall vs Preston: Preview, Predictions & Key Stats
evidences.content: Preston North End, on the other hand, have been in fine fettle, securing three consecutive wins following their recent loss to Birmingham. Their attacking play has been impressive, with some good goals in their recent victories. They'll be keen to stick to their winning ways and continue their push up the table. Look for Preston to press high and try to exploit any gaps in Millwall's defence, making for an exciting, end-to-end encounter. ## Form Guide & Team Overview ### Millwall [...] ## Form Guide & Team Overview ### Millwall Millwall's recent form has been a mixed bag, with a heavy defeat to Birmingham City sandwiched between a draw and three wins. Their home form has been particularly strong, with victories against Leicester and Stoke, which bodes well for this match. The loss to Birmingham might have dented their confidence, but playing at home could help them regain their footing. They'll need to be solid at the back and clinical in front of goal to overcome a confident Preston side. evidences.title: Millwall looking to end 36-year wait for top-flight football - BBC Sport
evidences.content: Five years later they came back up after claiming the Division Two title and then finished fourth the following campaign, but once again tasted defeat in at the play-off semi-final stage, by Birmingham City. In 2003-04 the most unlikely of FA Cup runs under Dennis Wise culminated in a final against Sir Alex Ferguson's Manchester United in Cardiff, with the Red Devils running out 3-0 winners through goals from Ruud van Nistelrooy and a young Portuguese winger playing in his first season in England, named Cristiano Ronaldo. Twice more, Millwall returned to League One and on both occasions won promotion through the play-offs, with Steve Morison scoring a late winner to down Bradford City in the 2016-17 final at Wembley. [...] Having finished third in the Championship, just one point behind runners up Ipswich Town, the Lions face a double-header against Hull City - with a visit to East Yorkshire on Friday - before a potential play-off final against Southampton or Middlesbrough for a place in the Premier League. "We're really confident in what we're doing, I think we believe more importantly that we're capable of going anywhere and winning games," Millwall head coach Neil told BBC Ra...
